behaviors.description: Required patient fields are validated before downstream processing; missing fields return a complete missing-items error.
behaviors.justification: Tournament: Unit vs Integration -> Integration wins because the failure boundary is validator-to-pipeline handoff (must prove downstream steps are not invoked). Integration vs System -> Integration wins because behavior text only requires call-gating and error aggregation, not real OS/network/subprocess effects.
behaviors.test_description: Run intake orchestration with missing `name` and `insurance_id`; assert response lists both missing fields and assert insurance/triage/scheduling modules are not called.

behaviors.description: DOB validation rejects future dates, >150-year-old dates, and non-date strings.
behaviors.justification: Tournament: Unit vs Integration -> Unit wins because behavior is pure date-rule parsing/validation in one local boundary. Rejection gate for higher levels: behavior text contains no module handoff, I/O, or model dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Directly test DOB validator with representative invalid/valid inputs and assert specific rejection reasons.

behaviors.description: Insurance eligibility check sends correct patient ID/plan code and maps eligible/not eligible/pending correctly.
behaviors.justification: Tournament: Unit vs Integration -> Integration wins because request-building + API-client + response-mapping interaction is the failure boundary. Integration vs System -> Integration wins; text asks payload correctness/status interpretation, not real network behavior. Rejected System due concrete text: `sends the correct ... and correctly interprets` (contract logic).
behaviors.test_description: Wire service to a stub payer client; verify outgoing payload fields and mapping for three returned statuses.

behaviors.description: If payer API is unreachable, eligibility becomes pending and intake continues.
behaviors.justification: Tournament: Unit vs Integration -> Integration wins because fallback state must propagate from payer-client failure into pipeline continuation. Integration vs System -> Integration wins since an injected connection-error from client abstraction reproduces this failure; behavior text does not require real socket/timeout semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Stub payer client to throw unreachable error; assert eligibility set to `pending` and subsequent triage/scheduling steps still execute.

behaviors.description: Triage call payload includes reported symptoms and configured emergency rubric.
behaviors.justification: Tournament: Unit vs Integration -> Integration wins because config + intake data + model-client adapter must be wired to form payload. Integration vs System -> Integration wins (no real network needed). System vs Agentic -> Integration/Agentic brake: this behavior tests request composition only, not model judgment quality, so Agentic is disqualified by text `includes ... in payload`.
behaviors.test_description: Run triage service with known symptoms and rubric config against a fake model endpoint/client; assert outgoing payload contains both sections exactly.

behaviors.description: Live AI triage marks emergency symptom combinations as high urgency.
behaviors.justification: Tournament: Unit vs Integration -> Integration wins initially (service+model call). Integration vs System -> System wins because real external model call is part of behavior (`live AI`). System vs Agentic -> Agentic wins because correctness depends on model decision quality for symptom interpretation. Rejected Workflow: behavior text describes one model decision, not multiple agentic stages.
behaviors.test_description: Execute triage against the real AI engine on emergency symptom bundles (e.g., chest pain + shortness of breath) over repeated runs; assert high-urgency rate meets threshold.

behaviors.description: Triage parser stores a clinician-readable explanation field, not only numeric score.
behaviors.justification: Tournament: Unit vs Integration -> Unit wins because this is parser/output-shape logic in a local boundary. Rejection gate: no behavior text requiring real model call, network, or multi-module orchestration beyond field extraction.
behaviors.test_description: Feed parser a representative triage response and assert persisted object includes non-empty `explanation` plus score.

behaviors.description: Live AI triage explanation is case-specific and includes symptom-based rationale, not boilerplate.
behaviors.justification: Tournament: Unit vs Integration -> Integration wins initially. Integration vs System -> System wins (`live AI` call). System vs Agentic -> Agentic wins because assertion is about explanation quality/content produced by model. Rejected Workflow due behavior text focusing on single triage generation step.
behaviors.test_description: Run real triage on multiple symptom sets; score explanations for symptom mention and rationale specificity, and fail if outputs are generic across distinct cases.

behaviors.description: Scheduling chooses first available slot matching urgency SLA (high same-day, medium within 48h).
behaviors.justification: Tournament: Unit vs Integration -> Integration wins because availability lookup + urgency policy + calendar write must interact. Integration vs System -> Integration wins since local DB/repo fixture can expose ordering/SLA bugs without real OS/network.
behaviors.test_description: With seeded calendar data, run scheduler for high and medium urgency; assert earliest valid slot chosen and SLA windows respected.

behaviors.description: Concurrent intake processing must not double-book the same slot.
behaviors.justification: Tournament: Unit vs Integration -> Integration wins because race appears only across scheduler + shared calendar persistence boundary. Integration vs System -> Integration narrowly wins when using real transactional local DB in test harness; no external network/subprocess required by behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Launch two concurrent scheduling requests targeting same remaining slot against a transactional test DB; assert one booking succeeds and the other is rejected/retried to a different slot.

behaviors.description: Confirmation message contains required identity/appointment/provider fields and visit-type prep instructions.
behaviors.justification: Tournament: Unit vs Integration -> Unit wins because message composition/template selection can be validated at a single function/object boundary. Rejection gate for higher levels: behavior text is content assembly, not transport/delivery or external dependency behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Call confirmation formatter with fixture data for multiple visit types; assert required fields and correct instruction variant appear.

behaviors.description: Orchestration pipeline completes correctly when triage output is deterministic stub.
behaviors.justification: Tournament: Unit vs Integration -> Integration wins because this explicitly tests cross-step orchestration/state handoff. Integration vs System -> Integration wins because dependencies are stubbed/deterministic by behavior text. Integration brake checks: no real OS/network required; no real AI output dependence (`stubbed triage`); no multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end in-process pipeline with stubbed triage and controlled payer/calendar fixtures; assert ordered step completion and expected final artifacts.

behaviors.description: Full live intake pipeline completes for a real patient submission without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: Tournament: Unit vs Integration -> Integration wins (multi-module pipeline). Integration vs System -> System wins (real external payer/API behavior). System vs Agentic -> Agentic wins because pipeline includes live AI triage and completion can depend on real model output. Rejected Workflow: behavior describes one agentic decision stage (triage), not multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Execute production-like intake against live payer integration and live triage model for a real submission in a controlled environment; assert automatic completion through confirmation with no operator steps.
